comments.body: Plan-review PASS after 3 rounds (BLOCK 6 -> BLOCK 2 -> PASS). Blocks were reviewer factual errors about SSHSIG validity windows / sign I/O; remediated by pinning the empirically-confirmed mechanism (stdin->stdout sign, -s sigfile verify, allowed_signers valid-before enforcement requiring >=8.9) into the ACs. Residual 19 advisories = recurring premature-abstraction cluster (epic-level decision) + test nitpicks, non-blocking. Proceeding to TDD.

compiled_state.description:
## Context
The first **asymmetric** scheme for the attest substrate (epic brilliant-curly-songbird): sign/verify over attestation bytes via OpenSSH `ssh-keygen -Y` (stdlib `subprocess`, **no new core dependency**), verified offline against an `allowed_signers`-format trust input, reusing developers existing SSH keys. Plugs into the registry `Scheme` protocol (task 518b). The `src/rebar/attest/` package already exists with `__init__.py` (task 9fd5).

## Mechanism (each command below was run and its behavior confirmed in this env, OpenSSH 10.2)
- **Sign — stdin -> stdout (never a file operand).** `ssh-keygen -Y sign -f <privkey> -n <namespace>` with the payload piped on **stdin** and **NO file operand** writes the armored SSHSIG to **stdout**. Confirmed: `printf ... | ssh-keygen -Y sign -f id -n ns > out.sig` produces a signature that `-Y verify` accepts. (The `<file>.sig` on-disk output the man page describes applies ONLY when a file operand is passed on argv; this code passes the payload on stdin and never a file operand, so the signature is always on stdout.) `sign(pae_bytes, key_path, namespace)` returns those stdout bytes.
- **Verify — sig file on disk, data on stdin.** `ssh-keygen -Y verify -f <allowed_signers_file> -I <principal> -n <namespace> -s <sig_file>` reads the payload on **stdin**; the signature must be a file (`-s`). The allowed_signers content and the signature bytes are written to temp files; the process branches **only on exit code** (0 = valid). Argv array, never a shell string.
- **Validity windows.** Enforced via `valid-before=`/`valid-after=` options on the `allowed_signers` line; `ssh-keygen -Y verify` honors them against current time (or `-Overify-time`). Confirmed: an `allowed_signers` line with `valid-before=20200101` makes verify exit non-zero. This support requires **OpenSSH >= 8.9** — the reason for the version floor (the `-Y sign/verify` subcommands exist since 8.0, but allowed_signers validity intervals landed in 8.9).
- **Never** use `-Y check-novalidate` (structure-only) for authorization.

## Files
- `src/rebar/attest/sshsig.py` — `ssh_keygen_version()`, `ensure_available()`, `sign()`, `SshsigScheme` (registry `Scheme`; `trust_root`=allowed_signers content, `signatures[0].keyid`=principal, `signatures[0].sig`=armored SSHSIG bytes), `register_sshsig_scheme()`.
- `src/rebar/attest/__init__.py` — call `register_sshsig_scheme()` at import.
- `tests/unit/test_attest_sshsig.py`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `SshsigScheme.verify(pae_bytes, signatures, namespace, trust_root)` invokes `ssh-keygen -Y verify` via an **argv array** (no shell), feeds `pae_bytes` on stdin, writes `trust_root` (allowed_signers) and `signatures[0].sig` to temp files, allowlist-validates principal + namespace (non-empty, no control chars), and returns a `registry.Verdict` branching **only** on exit code (0 -> `certified`, non-zero -> `mismatch`).
- [ ] Fail-closed test matrix (real `ssh-keygen`): `verified is False` on **tampered bytes**, **wrong namespace**, **unknown principal**, **substituted key**, and **expired validity window** (`valid-before` in the past). Plus a happy round-trip: `sign()` then `verify()` -> `certified`.
- [ ] `ssh_keygen_version()` returns the OpenSSH `(major, minor)` (or `None` if absent/unparseable). `SshsigScheme.verify` **fails closed** (non-`certified` `Verdict`, never a silent pass) when version detection returns `None` — test monkeypatches it to `None` and asserts `verified is False`.
- [ ] `ensure_available()` raises `SshKeygenUnavailable` (clear message) when ssh-keygen is absent (`ssh_keygen_version()` is `None`) OR below `8.9` — a test monkeypatches version detection to `None` and to `(8, 1)` and asserts `SshKeygenUnavailable` is raised in both cases.
- [ ] `register_sshsig_scheme()` registers `SshsigScheme` into the registry (discoverable via `registry.get_scheme("sshsig")`); no POLICY kinds pinned here (authorship kinds are the identity epic).
compiled_state.title: attest: SSHSIG scheme via ssh-keygen (fail-closed subprocess)
